people.name: Sophronius I, Bishop of Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Sophronius, Patriarch of Jerusalem
people.summary: The seventh-century hierarch who at the surrender of Jerusalem to the Caliph Umar received from him the covenant protecting the holy places, and lived just long enough to grieve the loss before his repose.

people.name: Sositheus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Sossios and Theokleios
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Sosthenes, Apollos, Cephas, Caesar, and Epaphroditus of the Seventy
people.summary: Five of the Seventy named by Paul in his epistles, sent through the early decades to the great cities of the empire.

people.name: Sosthenes, Apollos, Cephas, Tychicus, Epaphroditus, Caesar, and Onesiphorus of the Seventy
people.summary: Seven of the Seventy whom Paul names in his epistles, sent through the early decades to the great cities of the empire.

people.name: Sotiricus and Valentinos
people.summary: Two believers of Asia Minor dragged together over sharp stones for their confession of Christ.

people.name: St. John Chrysostom
people.summary: Thirty years after his death in exile at Comana, the body of Chrysostom was brought back to Constantinople by his disciple-emperor Theodosius II, who laid his cheek against the coffin and asked forgiveness in the name of his mother Eudoxia.

people.name: Forefeast of the Nativity of the Theotokos
people.summary: The eve of the Nativity of the Mother of God. Also commemorated: Sozon, a shepherd-boy of Cilicia who broke a great golden idol and gave its fragments to the poor, and under Maximian gave up his soul in the flames.

people.name: Sozon of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Speusippus, Eleusippus, Melapsippus, and their grandmother Leonilla of Cappadocia
people.summary: Three Cappadocian brothers — triplets — and their grandmother who brought them up in the faith, all crowned together in the second century.

people.name: Spyridon the Wonderworker, Bishop of Trimythous
people.summary: A fourth-century shepherd-bishop of Cyprus whose simple faith at the Council of Nicaea is said to have confounded a philosopher and converted him by the parable of the brick — fire, water, and clay from the one tile, as the Trinity from the one God.

people.name: St. Seraphim of Sarov
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stachys, Bishop of Hierapolis
people.summary: An early disciple ordained at Hierapolis by Bartholomew after the martyrdom of the apostle Philip.

people.name: Stephen, Abbot of Chenolakkos Monastery
people.summary: Eighth-century abbot of a monastery near Chalcedon, exiled and beaten for his confession of the holy icons.

people.name: Stephen and Anthimus, Priests of the Monastery of the Unsleeping Ones
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stephen I, King of Hungary
people.summary: Tenth- and eleventh-century king of Hungary who brought his nation into the Christian faith.

people.name: Stephen I, Pope of Rome
people.summary: Mid-third-century bishop of Rome beheaded on his throne while celebrating the Mass under Valerian.

people.name: Stephen II, Patriarch of Antioch
people.summary: A fifth-century patriarch of Antioch who, having confessed the faith of Chalcedon against the monophysite party, was cast into the Orontes.

people.name: Stephen II, Patriarch of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stephen of Mar Saba the Hymnographer
people.summary: An eighth-century monk of the great lavra of Mar Saba, nephew of John of Damascus, author of many of the canons in the Octoechos.

people.name: Stephen of Placidian
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stephen the Confessor, Archbishop of Surozh in Crimea
people.summary: An eighth-century archbishop of Surozh in the Crimea, a fearless confessor of the icons under the iconoclast Constantine V.

people.name: Stephen the Confessor, Wonderworker of Tryglia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stephen the Monk of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stephen the New of Mt. St. Auxentius
people.summary: An eighth-century monk of Bithynia, one of the great confessors of the icons under Constantine V Copronymus, dragged through the streets of Constantinople by a mob and beaten to death.

people.name: Stephen the New Light of Constantinople
people.summary: A tenth-century ascetic of Constantinople whose holy life shone like a new lamp before the city in the days after the iconoclast struggle.

people.name: Stephen the New, Patriarch of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stephen the Sabbaite the Hymnographer
people.summary: An eighth- and ninth-century monk of the Great Lavra of Mar Saba, nephew of John of Damascus, whose hymns fill the Octoechos.

people.name: Stephen the Wonderworker
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stephen the Wonderworker of Ascalon
people.summary: An eighth-century anchorite of Palestine who at the monastery of St. Sabbas spent his final years in silence and prayer.

people.name: Straton of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Straton, Philip, Eutychian, and Cyprian of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stratonicus and Mardonius
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Stylianos of Paphlagonia
people.summary: A sixth-century anchorite of Paphlagonia who from his youth was a friend and protector of children, often called upon for their healing.

people.name: Styracius
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Sukia and his nineteen companions of Armenia
people.summary: Twenty believers brought from Georgia and Armenia to martyrdom in the days of the apostolic preaching in the east.

people.name: Susanna the Deaconess of Palestine
people.summary: A fourth-century maiden of Palestine who under the name Joanna entered a male monastery and at her revelation was crowned for her confession.

people.name: Sylvester, Pope of Rome
people.summary: The fourth-century Bishop of Rome who through prayer healed the emperor Constantine of leprosy and baptized him; he stood as a quiet pillar through the years of the First Ecumenical Council.

people.name: Sylvia of Rome, mother of Gregory the Dialogist
people.summary: Sixth-century Roman matron and mother of Pope Gregory the Great, who outlived her husband to live as a recluse.

people.name: Symeon and Theodore, Founders of the Mega Spilaion Monastery, with the Shepherdess Euphrosyne
people.summary: Two fourth-century brothers who, led by the shepherdess Euphrosyne to a cave-icon of the Mother of God painted by St. Luke, founded the Great Cave Monastery in the Peloponnese.

people.name: Symeon, kinsman of the Lord, second Bishop of Jerusalem
people.summary: Son of Clopas the brother of Joseph the Betrothed, who succeeded James as bishop of Jerusalem after his martyrdom. He was crucified under Trajan at the age of one hundred and twenty.

people.name: Symeon Metaphrastes
people.summary: Tenth-century logothete of Constantinople who compiled and reworked the great body of saints' lives that bears his name.

people.name: Symeon of Persia, with the martyrs of Persia
people.summary: A fourth-century bishop of Seleucia and Ctesiphon under the Persian Sapor II, beheaded with a hundred and fifty fellow Christians the day after Pascha for refusing to gather tax from the Christians or worship the sun.

people.name: Symeon of Sinai
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Symeon of Syracuse
people.summary: An eleventh-century Sicilian monk and pilgrim who passed through Sinai, the Holy Land, and Rome to end his days as a hermit at Trier.

people.name: Symeon Stylites III of Cilicia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Symeon Stylites of Lesbos
people.summary: A ninth-century stylite of Lesbos who continued in his own land the asceticism of his Syrian namesake.

people.name: Symeon Stylites the Younger of Sicily
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Symeon the Ancient of Mount Sinai
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Symeon the Archimandrite, Stylite
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Symeon the New Theologian
people.summary: Eleventh-century abbot of St. Mamas at Constantinople, whose hymns and discourses on the conscious experience of divine light shape Orthodox spirituality to this day.

people.name: Symeon the Studite
people.summary: Tenth-century elder of the Stoudios monastery in Constantinople, spiritual father of Symeon the New Theologian.

people.name: Symeon the Stylite of the Wonderful Mountain
people.summary: The sixth-century Antiochene ascetic who lived sixty-eight years on a pillar near Antioch, gathering crowds with his prophetic counsel and miracles of healing.

people.name: Symeon the Stylite the Elder
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Syncletica of Alexandria
people.summary: Fourth-century desert mother of Alexandria, the foundress of a community of consecrated virgins whose sayings stand among the apothegms.

people.name: Synesius, Bishop of Karpasia in Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Synesius (Synetus)
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Synesius the Ascetic of Lysos in Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Synesius the Wonderworker, Bishop of Karpasia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Syrian Martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tadros of Edessa with Michael the Sabbaite
people.summary: Seventh-century bishop of Edessa and a monk of Mar Saba slain together at Jerusalem by the Saracens.

people.name: Talida, Abbess of Antinoë
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tarasius, Archbishop of Constantinople
people.summary: A ninth-century patriarch who under the Empress Irene presided over the Seventh Ecumenical Council at Nicaea, which restored the holy icons to the Church.

people.name: Tarcisius of Rome
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tatiana of Rome
people.summary: A noble deaconess of third-century Rome under Severus who endured the breaking of her teeth, the rending of her flesh, and the spectacle of the arena; the very lions licked her feet before she was beheaded.

people.name: Tation of Claudiopolis in Bithynia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Ten Martyrs of Crete
people.summary: Ten Christians of various ages from the towns of Crete under Decius who, sent up to the governor's tribunal at Gortyna, were beheaded together at the place still called Hagioi Deka.

people.name: Terence, Africanus, Maximus, Pompeius, and the thirty-six companions of Carthage
people.summary: Forty Christians of Carthage shut up with snakes and scorpions and at last beheaded under Decius.

people.name: Terence, Vincent, Emilian, and Bebaia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tertius of Iconium of the Seventy
people.summary: The scribe of the Letter to the Romans, who named himself in his master's farewells at the close of the epistle.

people.name: Tervel, Khan of Bulgaria
people.summary: The early-eighth-century Bulgarian khan baptized into the faith who came to the defense of Constantinople against the Arab siege of 718.

people.name: Thaddeus the Confessor of the Stoudios Monastery
people.summary: A ninth-century monk of the Stoudios who suffered for the cause of the holy icons under the second iconoclasm.

people.name: Thaïs of Egypt, the former harlot
people.summary: A fourth-century courtesan of Egypt converted by the elder Paphnutius, who enclosed her three years in a cell of bricks and at her repose saw her soul carried up.

people.name: Thalassius and Limneus of Cyrrhus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thalassius the Myrrh-gusher of Libya
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thalelæus of Gabala in Syria
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thalleleus
people.summary: A young physician of Cilicia in the third century who healed the sick without payment and was beheaded for refusing to sacrifice. The model for the unmercenary saints.

people.name: Thathuil
people.summary: An early Christian hanged from an apple tree for the name of Christ.

people.name: Thathuil and Bebaia of Edessa
people.summary: Brother and sister of second-century Edessa martyred together for the name of Christ.

people.name: Thaumastos the Wonderworker
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Adoration of the Magi: Melchior, Caspar, and Balthazar
people.summary: The wise men from the east who followed the star to the cave at Bethlehem and offered their gold, frankincense, and myrrh to the King, the God, and the One who was to die for us.

people.name: the Blind Man, healed by St. Panteleimon
people.summary: The blind man whose sight Panteleimon restored and who confessed Christ openly at the saint's trial, sharing in his beheading.

people.name: The Commemoration of the Shepherds
people.summary: The shepherds keeping watch over their flocks by night to whom the angel announced great joy and a multitude of the heavenly host sang glory in the highest.

people.name: The Eight Holy Anchorites
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Eight Martyrs of Nicaea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Eighty Holy Martyrs of Byzantium
people.summary: Eighty priests and clergy of fourth-century Constantinople burned alive in the ship that bore them into exile by the Arian Valens.

people.name: The Fifteen Thousand Martyrs of Pisidia
people.summary: A great company converted at the witness of St. Marina of Antioch and beheaded together.

people.name: The five hundred men and one hundred and thirty women martyred with Juliana
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Forty Children-Martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Forty-four Monk-martyrs of Mar Saba
people.summary: Monks of the Great Lavra of St. Sabbas in Palestine slain together by Saracen raiders in 614.

people.name: The Four Crowned Martyrs: Claudius, Castor, Sempronian, and Nicostratus of Pannonia
people.summary: Four sculptors of fourth-century Pannonia who refused to carve an idol of Aesculapius and were beaten to death together for their confession of Christ.

people.name: The Four Fellow Ascetics of Dius
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Four Martyrs of Perge in Pamphylia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Holy Martyr formerly an Executioner
people.summary: An imperial executioner who at the spectacle of his prisoner's patience confessed Christ on the spot and was slain with the very sword by which he had served.

people.name: The Holy Martyrs of Lyon: Pothinus, Sanctus, Maturus, Attalus, Blandina, and companions
people.summary: A great company of believers of Gaul slain together in the amphitheater of Lyon in 177 — their letter to the Asian churches preserves the earliest record of Christian martyrdom in the West.

people.name: The Holy Martyrs of Mamilla
people.summary: A great company of Christians of Jerusalem slain in the Mamilla pool by the Persians at the sack of the city in 614.

people.name: The Holy Nine Martyrs of Perge in Pamphylia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Holy Nine Martyrs of Rome: Abundius, Alexander, Antigonus, Calanus, Januarius, Makarios, Severianus, Titianus, and Fortunatus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Holy One Hundred and Fifty Orators of Alexandria
people.summary: The philosophers and rhetoricians converted by Catherine's words and given over together to the fire.

people.name: the Holy Prophet Zechariah and the Righteous Elizabeth
people.summary: The aged priest in the temple and his barren wife to whom Gabriel announced the birth of the Forerunner.

people.name: The Holy Senators of Sebasteia: Eudoxios, Agapios, Atticus, Marinus, Oceanus, Eustratios, Karterios, Nikopolitianos, Styrax, and Tobias
people.summary: A company of noble Christians of Sebaste in Armenia burned together for the name of Christ under Licinius.

people.name: The Holy Soldier-Martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Martyrs of Adrianople
people.summary: A company of believers slain at Adrianople during the Bulgar invasions of the early ninth century.

people.name: The Martyrs of Niculitsel, with Hieromartyr Apotacius and thirty-seven companions
people.summary: A fourth-century company of Christians slain together at Noviodunum on the Danube, whose relics were found at Niculitsel in modern Romania.

people.name: The Nine Children-Martyrs of Kola in Georgia
people.summary: Nine Georgian children of the sixth century stoned by their pagan kinsmen for receiving baptism: Guram, Adarnase, Baqar, Vache, Bardzim, Dachi, Dzhuansher, Ramaz, and Parsman.

people.name: The Nine Holy Martyrs of Persia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The One Hundred and Fifty Philosophers of Alexandria
people.summary: The pagan rhetoricians and philosophers gathered by Maximinus Daia to dispute with St. Catherine, who were converted by her words and given up to the fire together.

people.name: The One Hundred and Fifty-six Martyrs of Palestine
people.summary: A great company of believers — bishops, clergy, monastics, and laity — slain in Palestine in the persecution of Maximinus Daia.

people.name: The One Hundred and Fifty-three Martyrs of Thrace
people.summary: A great company of Christians of Thrace drowned together for the name of Christ.

people.name: The One Hundred and Twenty Martyrs of Persia
people.summary: A great company of believers of Persia slain together under Sapor II for the name of Christ.

people.name: The One Thousand and Three Martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: A great company of believers of Nicomedia slain together under Diocletian for the name of Christ.

people.name: The One Thousand Four Hundred and Eighty Martyrs of Samaria
people.summary: A great company of Christians of Sebasteia in Palestine slain by the Persians at the sack of the city in 614.

people.name: The Seven Apostolic Men of Spain: Torquatus, Ctesiphon, Secundus, Indaletius, Caecilius, Hesychius, and Euphrasius
people.summary: Seven bishops ordained by Peter and Paul and sent to plant the Iberian church.

people.name: The Seven Former Thieves, with Cercyra of Corfu
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seven Holy Maccabees, their mother Solomonia, and their teacher Eleazar
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seven Holy Martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seven Holy Youths (Seven Sleepers) of Ephesus
people.summary: Seven young men of third-century Ephesus walled up in a cave under Decius who awoke two centuries later as a sign of the resurrection of the dead.

people.name: The Seven Martyrs of Samosata: Romanus, James, Philotheus, and their companions
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seven Virgin-martyrs of Gaza
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seven Women-Martyrs and Two Youths with St. Blaise of Sebaste
people.summary: Followers of the bishop who at his witness confessed Christ and shared in his crown at Sebaste.

people.name: The Seven Youths by name: Maximilian, Jamblicus, Martinian, John, Dionysius, Exacustodian, and Antoninus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seventeen Monk-martyrs of India
people.summary: A community of fourth-century Christian ascetics in India slain together for the name of Christ by the pagans of the country.

people.name: The Seventy Apostles
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seventy Martyrs of Scythopolis
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Seventy-three Martyrs of Sirmium
people.summary: A great company of believers of Sirmium in Pannonia slain together under Diocletian.

people.name: The Six Holy Martyrs of Bizin
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Six Martyrs of Melitene
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Sixteen Monk-martyrs of Thebes
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Sixty-three Martyrs of Jerusalem
people.summary: A great company of clergy and monastics of Jerusalem slain together for the name of Christ in 724.

people.name: The Ten Holy Martyrs of Egypt
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Ten Holy Martyrs of Halki
people.summary: Eighth-century defenders of the bronze icon of the Lord at the Chalke gate of Constantinople, beheaded by the iconoclast Leo III the Isaurian.

people.name: The Ten Holy Virgin-martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: A company of consecrated virgins of fourth-century Nicomedia slain together under Maximian.

people.name: The Ten Martyrs by name: Theodulus, Saturninus, Euporus, Gelasius, Eunician, Zoticus, Pompeius, Agathopus, Basilides, and Evaristus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Ten Martyrs of Gaza
people.summary: Ten believers of seventh-century Gaza slain together by the Saracens in the early years of the conquest.

people.name: The Ten Martyrs of Phoenicia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Ten Martyrs of Tomis: Paul, Cyriacus, Paula, Felicilana, Thomas, Felix, Martyrius, Vitaly, Crispinus, and Emilius
people.summary: Ten believers of Tomis on the Black Sea coast slain together for the name of Christ.

people.name: The Ten Thousand Ascetics of the Thebaid
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Ten Thousand Martyrs of Antioch
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Ten Thousand Martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Ten Thousand Venerable Fathers of Nitria
people.summary: A great company of monks of the Egyptian desert dispersed under the patriarch Theophilus of Alexandria in the Origenist crisis.

people.name: The Ten Venerable Ascetics of Egypt
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Thirty-eight Martyrs of the Bath House
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Thirty-three Martyrs of Heraclea in Elis
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Thirty-three Martyrs of Palestine
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Three Hundred and Sixty-six Martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Three Hundred and Sixty-two Martyrs of Africa
people.summary: Sixty-two priests and three hundred laypeople of fifth-century North Africa slain together by the Arian Vandals under Hunneric.

people.name: The Three Hundred Holy Paupers, the Companions of Florus and Laurus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Three Martyrs of Melitene
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Three Thousand Six Hundred and Twenty-Eight Martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: A vast company of believers slain together at Nicomedia under Diocletian.

people.name: The Twelve Soldier-Martyrs of Crete
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Twenty-five Martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Twenty-seven Holy Martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Twenty-six Martyrs of Gothia
people.summary: Twenty-six Crimean Gothic Christians, including catechumens and clergy, burned together in their wooden church under king Jungerich.

people.name: The twenty-three companions of Adrian and Natalia of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Two Ascetic Hermits
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Two Holy Martyrs of Parium
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Two Hundred and Fifty Martyrs of Bulgaria
people.summary: A great company of Byzantine Christians killed by Khan Krum and his successors after the capture of Adrianople and Berroia.

people.name: The Two Thousand Martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: The Virgin-martyrs of Emesa
people.summary: A company of consecrated maidens of Emesa in Syria slain together for the name of Christ in 779 during the Abbasid persecution.

people.name: Protomartyr and Equal-to-the-Apostles Thecla
people.summary: A noblewoman of Iconium converted by the apostle Paul whose witness she followed through fire, beasts, and exile; she labored as an evangelist and reposed in old age in a cave at Seleucia.

people.name: Themistocles of Myra
people.summary: A young shepherd of Lycia who at the soldiers' search for the Christian Dioscorus came forward in his place and was given over to torments and death.

people.name: Theocles and Valerianus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theocleta the Wonderworker
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoclite the Wonderworker
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoclitus the former Sorcerer
people.summary: A magician of the imperial household who at the witness of Photini was converted and shared in the company's crown.

people.name: Theocteristus, Abbot of Mount Olympus
people.summary: An eighth-century abbot of a monastery on Mount Olympus in Bithynia who suffered exile for the cause of the holy icons.

people.name: Theoctiste of the Hundred Doors of Lesbos
people.summary: A ninth-century maiden of Lesbos who lived hidden for many years on the deserted island of Paros after fleeing Saracen captors.

people.name: Theoctistus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoctistus, abbot of Cucumo in Sicily
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoctistus of Nicomedia
people.summary: A young Christian of Nicomedia beheaded with St. Cyprian and St. Justina for the name of Christ.

people.name: Theoctistus of Palestine
people.summary: A fifth-century anchorite of the Judean desert and companion of Euthymius the Great, abbot of the first lavra they founded together.

people.name: Theoctistus of Tekoa in Palestine
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoctistus the Patrician
people.summary: A ninth-century imperial official confessor of the holy icons under the second iconoclasm.

people.name: Theoctistus, Theagenes, and Theoteknos
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodora and Theopisti of Thessaloniki
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodora of Alexandria
people.summary: A fifth-century noblewoman who in repentance for a single sin disguised herself as a man and lived hidden as a monk in a male monastery for many years, her secret revealed only at her repose.

people.name: Theodora of Caesarea
people.summary: Eighth-century Cappadocian noblewoman who at her parents' urging took the monastic habit at Caesarea.

people.name: Theodora of Constantinople
people.summary: A tenth-century empress of New Rome who at the end of her life took the monastic habit.

people.name: Theodora of Thessaloniki
people.summary: Late-ninth-century nun of Thessaloniki whose body was found incorrupt and worked many wonders.

people.name: Theodora of Thessaloniki and Aegina
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodora the Empress
people.summary: Ninth-century empress of New Rome, wife of the iconoclast Theophilus, who at her widowhood restored the holy icons through the synod of 843.

people.name: Theodore and George
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore and Martyr Pausilippus of Thrace
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore and Oceanus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore, Bishop of Cyrene in Libya
people.summary: Early-fourth-century bishop of Cyrene tortured and beheaded with the maidens Cyprilla, Aroa, and Lucia.

people.name: Theodore, Bishop of Edessa, the Savvaitis
people.summary: A ninth-century Syrian monk of Mar Saba raised to the see of Edessa, who labored for the conversion of his people.

people.name: Theodore, Bishop of Theodosiopolis
people.summary: A late-sixth-century bishop of Theodosiopolis (Erzurum) in Armenia.

people.name: Theodore, Bishop of Tyre
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore, Euprepius, and the two Anastasii, disciples of Maximus the Confessor
people.summary: Seventh-century confessors who shared with St. Maximus his last sufferings under the monothelite emperor Constans II.

people.name: Theodore, founder of the Monastery of Chora
people.summary: A sixth-century founder of the Chora monastery at the walls of Constantinople, whose church holds some of the finest Byzantine mosaics.

people.name: Theodore Graptus the Branded
people.summary: A ninth-century confessor of the icons whose forehead was burned with iambic verses by the iconoclast Theophilus, who at last reposed in exile before his brother.

people.name: Theodore I, Patriarch of Alexandria
people.summary: Early-seventh-century patriarch of Alexandria slain by the Persians for refusing the worship of fire.

people.name: Theodore I, Patriarch of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore of Antioch
people.summary: A Christian of Antioch slain in the years just after Julian the Apostate for the name of Christ.

people.name: Theodore of Kiev and his son John, the Varangians
people.summary: A tenth-century Christian Varangian at Kiev and his son slain by the pagans for refusing to give the boy as a human sacrifice — the first martyrs of the Russian land.

people.name: Theodore of Perge, his mother Philippa, and their companions
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore of Perge, his mother Philippa, with Dioscorus, Socrates, and Dionysius
people.summary: A young Christian of Pamphylia and his mother who at his witness confessed Christ, along with three companions, all crowned together under Antoninus.

people.name: Theodore the Ascetic of Kythera
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore the Bishop of Cyrenia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore the Confessor, Abbot of the Stoudios Monastery
people.summary: Ninth-century abbot of the great Stoudios monastery of Constantinople, exiled three times by the iconoclasts and shaper of Byzantine cenobitic life.

people.name: Theodore the Confessor, Bishop of Ancyra
people.summary: A ninth-century bishop of Ancyra who suffered exile under the iconoclast emperors for the cause of the holy images.

people.name: Theodore the Fool-for-Christ
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore the General (Stratelates)
people.summary: A fourth-century officer of Heraclea on the Black Sea who under Licinius openly broke the city's idols, was crucified, pierced through with arrows, and beheaded — the elder of the two great Theodores.

people.name: Theodore the Sabbaite, Archbishop of Edessa
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore the Sanctified
people.summary: The favorite disciple of St. Pachomius and his successor as abbot of Tabennisi. The Church remembers him a day after his teacher.

people.name: Theodore the Sykeote, Bishop of Anastasiopolis
people.summary: A sixth-century Galatian who from boyhood took up the monastic life, became a great wonderworker, was made bishop against his will, and reposed back in the small cave-cell that had been his first hermitage.

people.name: Great-martyr Theodore the Tyro (Stratelates)
people.summary: The fourth-century soldier-martyr's body, having been returned to his native Euchaita after his passion, was translated with great honor and continued to work signs at his shrine.

people.name: Theodore the Wonderworker of Tamassos in Cyprus
people.summary: A second-century bishop of Tamassos in Cyprus renowned for his miracles.

people.name: Theodore the Wonderworker of the Jordan
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodore Trichinas
people.summary: A fifth-century recluse of Constantinople who from his cell at Asomatos lived in such severe asceticism that his hair-shirt (whence his name) wore against his skin for forty years; many miracles followed at his shrine.

people.name: Theodore, with the Deacon Irenaeus, of Pentapolis in Libya
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodoret of Antioch
people.summary: A fourth-century priest of Antioch beheaded under Julian the Apostate for refusing to surrender the church treasures.

people.name: Theodoretus of Antioch
people.summary: A fourth-century priest of Antioch beheaded under Julian the Apostate for refusing to surrender the church treasures.

people.name: Theodoretus the Presbyter of Antioch
people.summary: A fourth-century priest of Antioch beheaded under Julian the Apostate.

people.name: Theodorus and Archimandrite Agapius of Apamea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodosia, mother of Procopius
people.summary: The mother of Great-martyr Procopius, converted by his witness and beheaded with him.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Theodosia of Tyre
people.summary: An eighteen-year-old of Tyre who in the year 308 approached condemned Christians to give them words of comfort, and was at once seized, tortured, and cast into the sea.

people.name: Theodosia the Princess and the Eunuch Gerontios
people.summary: A daughter of Emperor Hadrian and the chamberlain who served her, both honored as second-century saints.

people.name: Theodosius
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodosius, Abbot of Tryglia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodosius I the Great, Emperor of New Rome
people.summary: Late-fourth-century Christian Roman emperor who made the Nicene faith the religion of the empire and convened the Second Ecumenical Council.

people.name: Theodosius II the Younger, Emperor of New Rome
people.summary: Fifth-century pious emperor in whose reign John Chrysostom's relics were brought home to Constantinople and Pulcheria his sister ordered the affairs of the Church.

people.name: Theodosius of Skopelos in Cilicia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodosius of the Kiev Caves
people.summary: Eleventh-century abbot of the Kiev Caves Lavra and father of Russian cenobitic monasticism.

people.name: Theodosius the Great, founder of cenobitic monasticism
people.summary: The sixth-century abbot of the great lavra near Bethlehem whose rule organized communal monastic life in Palestine and whose stand against the monophysites at the Marian shrine called the whole desert to Orthodoxy.

people.name: Theodosius the New of Peloponnese
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodosius the Stylite of Edessa
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodosius the Wonderworker of Rhosus
people.summary: A fifth-century ascetic of Antioch and Rhosus on the Cilician coast renowned for miracles of healing.

people.name: Theodota
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodota and her three sons of Bithynia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodota, Evodus, Eutychianus, and Zoilos
people.summary: Companions and household of St. Anastasia who shared in her sufferings and her crowning.

people.name: Theodota, mother of Cosmas and Damian
people.summary: Pious widow of Asia Minor who raised her sons in the faith and taught them the practice of unmercenary medicine.

people.name: Theodota, mother of Cosmas and Damian of Asia
people.summary: Third-century widow of Asia Minor who raised her physician sons in the faith and the unmercenary practice.

people.name: Theodota of Nicaea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodota of Philippi
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodotus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodotus and Theodota, companions of Hyacinth
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodotus and Theodota of Caesarea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodotus, Bishop of Cyrene
people.summary: An early-fourth-century bishop of Cyrene in Libya slain under Licinius for his confession.

people.name: Theodotus, Bishop of Ephesus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodotus of Ancyra and the Seven Virgins
people.summary: An innkeeper of Ancyra who hid Christians during Diocletian's persecution; condemned for retrieving and burying the bodies of seven aged virgin-martyrs drowned in a lake.

people.name: Theodotus of Ancyra
people.summary: The fourth-century bishop of Ancyra in Galatia, beheaded under Maximian for confessing Christ and converting many through his preaching.

people.name: Theodotus, Bishop of Cyrene
people.summary: A fourth-century bishop of Cyrenaica under Licinius who, scourged and racked, was finally beheaded after his judge had himself converted at the spectacle of his patience.

people.name: Theodotus the Innkeeper of Ancyra
people.summary: An innkeeper of Ancyra who took up the bodies of the seven virgin-martyrs of his city for honorable burial and was beheaded for it.

people.name: Theodoulos of Caesarea in Palestine
people.summary: A fourth-century slave of the prefect Firmilian who having visited the imprisoned Christians of Caesarea openly confessed Christ and was crucified.

people.name: Theodoulos of Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theodoulos the Stylite
people.summary: A fifth-century patrician of Constantinople who renounced his rank under Theodosius the Younger and stood thirty years on a pillar near Ephesus.

people.name: Theodula of Anazarbus and her companions Helladius, Theodoulos, Boethius, Evagrius, and Macarius
people.summary: A maiden of fourth-century Anazarbus in Cilicia and her companions, all converted at her testimony and crowned together under Diocletian.

people.name: Theodula of Anazarbus, with Helladius, Macarius, Boethos, and Evagrius
people.summary: A maiden of fourth-century Anazarbus in Cilicia and her companions, all crowned together under Diocletian.

people.name: Theodulus the Reader and Agathopodes the Deacon
people.summary: A young reader and an aged deacon of fourth-century Thessalonica under Diocletian, bound together back-to-back and cast into the sea, where their bodies came ashore intact for burial.

people.name: Theodulus, son of Nilus of Sinai
people.summary: A young Christian of Sinai taken captive by Bedouins with the other children of the slaughtered fathers, sought out and recovered by his father Nilus.

people.name: Theogenes, Bishop of Parium
people.summary: An early-fourth-century bishop of Parium on the Hellespont, drowned in the sea for the name of Christ.

people.name: Theognius, Bishop of Bethelia near Gaza
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theokletos, Archbishop of Sparta
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoklitos
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoktistos of Mar Saba
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theonas, Symeon, and Fervinus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theonas, with the Two Children
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Eve of Theophany — Strict Fast
people.summary: A day of strict fasting and the Royal Hours of Theophany, when the Great Blessing of Waters is appointed. Also commemorated: Theopempt the bishop and Theonas the converted magician, beheaded under Diocletian.

people.name: Theopempt and Theonas of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theopemptus and his four companions
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theopemptus and the Six Martyrs of Phrygia
people.summary: Seven Christians of Phrygia given over together to the fire for their confession.

people.name: Gregory of Nyssa
people.summary: The fourth-century Cappadocian father, younger brother of Basil the Great, whose meditations on the soul's ascent to God remain among the deepest in the Christian tradition — 'every end is a new beginning.'

people.name: Theophanes, Archbishop of Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theophanes Graptus the Branded, Bishop of Nicaea
people.summary: A ninth-century confessor of the icons whose forehead was burned with iambic verses by the iconoclast Theophilus and who at the triumph was made bishop of Nicaea.

people.name: Theophanes of Antioch and Pansemne the Penitent
people.summary: A fourth-century monk of Antioch and the harlot whose conversion he sought out by living as a beggar at her door for three years.

people.name: Theophanes the Confessor and Faster of Mount Diabenos
people.summary: An ascetic of the third century renowned for the severity of his fasting and his confession of Christ before the persecutors.

people.name: Theophanes the Merciful of Gaza
people.summary: A holy man of Gaza known for spending all his substance upon the poor and for sheltering the homeless under his own roof.

people.name: Theophilos
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theophilos of Crete and his companions
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theophilos the Confessor, Archbishop of Ephesus
people.summary: A confessor of the icons under the second iconoclasm and shepherd of the Ephesian church in the ninth century.

people.name: Theophilus, Bishop of Caesarea in Palestine
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theophilus I, Pope of Alexandria
people.summary: Patriarch of Alexandria from 385 to 412 and uncle of Cyril, a vigorous defender of orthodoxy whose name stands at the head of many of the early canons.

people.name: Theophilus of Antioch
people.summary: Second-century apologist and bishop of Antioch whose work To Autolycus is one of the earliest extant writings of the post-apostolic Church.

people.name: Theophilus, Saturninus, and Revocatus of Spain
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theophilus the Confessor
people.summary: An eighth-century confessor of Constantinople who endured many tortures under the iconoclast emperors.

people.name: Theophilus the Deacon
people.summary: A deacon of Nicomedia who suffered together with St. Anthimus and many of the great Nicomedian church.

people.name: Theophilus the Deacon and Martyr Helladius of Libya
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theophilus the New of Cyprus
people.summary: An eighth-century Christian of Cyprus martyred under the iconoclast emperors.

people.name: Theophilus, Trophimus, and thirteen companions of Rome
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theophylact the Confessor, Bishop of Nicomedia
people.summary: A ninth-century friend of Tarasius of Constantinople and bishop of Nicomedia who under Leo the Armenian was driven from his see to thirty years of exile in a fortress on the Bosphorus for the icons.

people.name: Theopiste, Agapius, and Theopistus
people.summary: Wife and two sons of Eustathius, given over to the fire with him in the brazen ox at Rome.

people.name: Theopistus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoprobus, Bishop of Karpasia in Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theosebius the God-bearer of Arsinoe in Cyprus
people.summary: An ascetic of Cyprus remembered for his bearing of the Spirit and the gentleness of his counsel.

people.name: Theosterictus the Confessor of Pelekete
people.summary: Ninth-century abbot of the monastery of Pelekete near Prusa exiled for the holy icons.

people.name: Theotecnus of Antioch
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theoteknos and Martyrs Marianus and Eugenios
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theotima of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Theotimos and Theodoulos of Ephesus
people.summary: Two executioners converted by St. Hermione who themselves received the crown of martyrdom.

people.name: Theotimus, Bishop of Tomis in Moesia
people.summary: An early-fifth-century bishop of Tomis on the Black Sea coast whose holy life so impressed the Huns that they called him 'the god of the Romans.'

people.name: The Dormition of Our Most Holy Lady the Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary
people.summary: The end of the earthly life of the Mother of God. The apostles, gathered miraculously to her bedside, accompanied her body to Gethsemane; on the third day, when Thomas came, the tomb was found empty, for she had been taken up by her Son to the place He had prepared.

people.name: Therapon, Macarius, Marcius, and Marcia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Therapont, Bishop of Sardis
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Therapont of White Lake
people.summary: Fifteenth-century Russian monk of Belozersk and founder of monastic communities in the northern forests.

people.name: Therapontus, Bishop of Cyprus
people.summary: A seventh-century bishop of Cyprus slain by Arab raiders for the name of Christ.

people.name: Therissos, Bishop of Karpasia in Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Therissos (Thyrsos), Bishop of Karpasia in Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thermos (Fermus, Firmus)
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thespesius of Cappadocia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thomais of Alexandria
people.summary: A young Christian wife of fifth-century Alexandria slain by her own father-in-law for refusing his evil desires.

people.name: Thomais of Lesbos
people.summary: A tenth-century lay-saint of Lesbos who endured a long unhappy marriage with patience and almsgiving.

people.name: Thomas
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thomas, Abbot of the St. Euthymius Monastery
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thomas Dephourkinos of Mount Kyminas
people.summary: A tenth-century monk of Bithynia who at his great cell on Mount Kyminas received gifts of healing and clairvoyance.

people.name: Thomas I, Patriarch of Jerusalem
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thomas II, Patriarch of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thomas of Mount Maleon
people.summary: Tenth-century anchorite of Laconia in the Peloponnese.

people.name: Thomas, Patriarch of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thomas the Fool-for-Christ of Syria
people.summary: A sixth-century holy fool of Syria whose disciplined poverty drew the ridicule of all who did not see what he saw.

people.name: Three Children and their Mother, Martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Three Holy Martyrs of Galatia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Three Virgin-Martyrs of Chios
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thyrsus and Agnes of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thyrsus, Leucius, and Callinicus of Caesarea in Bithynia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Thyrsus, Leucius, Philemon, Apollonius, and Callinicus
people.summary: Five Christians of third-century Asia Minor under Decius — three killed at Caesarea in Bithynia, two at Alexandria — whose various crowns are joined in a single feast.

people.name: Tigrius the Presbyter and Eutropius the Lector of Constantinople
people.summary: A priest and a reader of Constantinople who suffered for John Chrysostom at his exile.

people.name: Tikhon the Wonderworker, Bishop of Amathus
people.summary: A fifth-century bishop of Cyprus, beloved by the poor and revered for his gift of healing and the steady wisdom by which he governed his see.

people.name: Timolaus and the Seven Companions of Caesarea
people.summary: Eight Christians of fourth-century Caesarea in Palestine who came forward together at the trial of the bishop and were beheaded with him.

people.name: Timon of the Seventy, the Deacon
people.summary: One of the seven deacons appointed by the apostles, who labored in Bostra and reposed in the fire after preaching there.

people.name: Timon the Hermit
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Timothy, Agapius, and Thecla of Palestine
people.summary: Three Christians of fourth-century Palestine slain under Maximinus Daia — Timothy at the slow fire, Thecla to the beasts, Agapius after long torments.

people.name: Timothy and Diogenes of Macedonia
people.summary: Two believers of fourth-century Macedonia slain by the Arians in the doctrinal struggles after Nicaea.

people.name: Timothy and Maura
people.summary: The reader Timothy and his wife Maura, newly wed, were martyred together in Egypt under Diocletian for refusing to surrender the Scriptures. Also commemorated: St. Theodosius of the Kiev Caves.

people.name: Timothy and Theodore, Bishops of Tiberiopolis, with the company slain with them
people.summary: A great host of bishops, priests, and monks of Tiberiopolis in Macedonia slain together under Julian the Apostate.

people.name: Timothy, Archbishop of Alexandria
people.summary: Late-fourth-century patriarch of Alexandria, brother of Peter II, who presided in the great see in the years after the Second Ecumenical Council.

people.name: Timothy of Proconnesus the Wonderworker
people.summary: A sixth-century archbishop of Proconnesus on the Sea of Marmara famed for his miracles.

people.name: Timothy, Bishop of Prussa
people.summary: A fourth-century bishop in Bithynia, beheaded by Julian the Apostate for refusing to recall his rebukes against the imperial relapse into paganism.

people.name: Timothy of the Seventy
people.summary: Timothy, the disciple of Paul and first bishop of Ephesus, who at the great Diana-feast rebuked the city and was stoned by the mob. Also commemorated: Anastasius, a Persian convert and former magus, beheaded under Khosrau II.

people.name: Timothy of Symbola
people.summary: A sixth-century monk of Bithynia who as a young man was a disciple of the elder Theoctistus and afterwards became himself an abbot, founding the monastery of Symbola at the foot of Mount Olympus.

people.name: Timothy the Hermit of Egypt
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Timothy the Monk of Caesarea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Timothy the Stylite of Kakhushta
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tiridates III, King of Armenia
people.summary: The fourth-century king brought to faith by Gregory the Illuminator who in 301 made Armenia the first Christian kingdom.

people.name: Tithoes
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tithoes of the Thebaid
people.summary: Fourth-century disciple of Pachomius the Great in the cenobia of upper Egypt.

people.name: Titus, Bishop of Bostra in Arabia
people.summary: A fourth-century bishop of Bostra renowned for his treatise against the Manichees.

people.name: Titus, Bishop of Byzantium
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Titus, Bishop of Tomis
people.summary: A fourth-century bishop of Tomis on the Black Sea, slain for his pastoral labors among the Goths.

people.name: Titus the Wonderworker
people.summary: A monk of the eighth century at a monastery near the Hellespont, known both for the gift of healing and for the meek silence that healed many more than his miracles did.

people.name: St. Nicephorus, Patriarch of Constantinople
people.summary: Nineteen years after his death in exile, the bones of the confessor Nicephorus were carried in triumph from his island monastery back to the city he had ruled before iconoclasm drove him out.

people.name: Triphyllius, Bishop of Leucosia in Cyprus
people.summary: Fourth-century bishop of Cyprus, friend and disciple of Spyridon of Trimythous, learned in the laws of the empire.

people.name: Triphyllius, Bishop of Nicosia in Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Troadius of Neo-Caesarea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Trophimus and Eucarpion of Nicomedia
people.summary: Two soldiers of fourth-century Nicomedia under Maximian who at the witness of their victims converted and were crowned with them.

people.name: Trophimus and Thalus, Priests of Laodicea
people.summary: Two priests of Laodicea crowned together for the name of Christ under Diocletian.

people.name: Trophimus, Sabbatius, and Dorymedon
people.summary: Three Christians of Antioch under Probus who refused the pagan sacrifices at Daphne; Sabbatius died under the lash, Trophimus and Dorymedon were beheaded together.

people.name: Trophimus, Theophilus, and thirteen with them
people.summary: Christians of Lycia under Diocletian who, after the breaking of their bones and being cast into prison, were beheaded together for refusing the imperial decree of sacrifice.

people.name: Tryfaini and Matrona of Cyzicus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tryphaena of Cyzicus
people.summary: A first-century maiden of Cyzicus given over to wild beasts for the name of Christ.

people.name: Tryphon of Lampsacus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tryphon of Pechenga, Enlightener of the Lapps
people.summary: Sixteenth-century missionary monk who founded the Pechenga monastery in the far north and brought the Lapps to baptism.

people.name: Tryphon, Patriarch of Constantinople
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Forefeast of the Meeting
people.summary: The eve of the Lord's Meeting in the Temple. Also commemorated: Tryphon, the gentle goose-herd of Phrygia who in his youth cast out a demon from a Roman princess and was beheaded under Decius at the age of eighteen.

people.name: Tryphon, Trophimus, Dorymedon, and the one hundred and fifty with them
people.summary: A great company of Christians of Palestine slain together for the name of Christ.

people.name: The 20,000 Martyrs of Nicomedia
people.summary: The great multitude of Christians of Nicomedia who, having gathered in the cathedral for the Nativity Liturgy under Maximian, were locked in by his order and burned together with the church about them.

people.name: Two Holy Children-Martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Two Holy Martyrs, father and son
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Two Holy Martyrs of Phrygia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Two Holy Martyrs of Thrace
people.summary: Two Christians of Thrace crushed together between great stones for their confession of Christ.

people.name: Two Martyrs of Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Two Martyrs of Tyre
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Tychicus of the Seventy
people.summary: An early disciple of Paul named in his letters as the apostle's faithful messenger, who at length became bishop of Chalcedon.

people.name: Tygrius the Presbyter and Eutropius the Lector of Constantinople
people.summary: A priest and a lector of Constantinople who suffered for John Chrysostom at his second exile.

people.name: Ulphianus of Tyre
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Urban, Theodore, Medimnus, and the seventy-seven priests and deacons of Nicomedia
people.summary: A company of clergy of Nicomedia under the Arian Valens, burned alive in the ship that bore them into exile.

people.name: Urpasian the Senator of Nicomedia
people.summary: An imperial senator of fourth-century Nicomedia burned alive under Maximian for the name of Christ.

people.name: Ursicius of Illyricum
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Vakhtang I Gorgasali, King of Iberia
people.summary: Fifth-century king of Iberia who freed his land from Persian overlordship and built churches across his realm.

people.name: Valentine, Bishop of Interamna, with the disciples Proculus, Ephebus, Apollonius, and Abundius
people.summary: Third-century bishop of Terni in Italy and his disciples, beheaded together for the name of Christ.

people.name: Valerius
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Vardan Mamikonian of Armenia
people.summary: Fifth-century Armenian commander who led his nation against Sasanian Persia at the battle of Avarayr and gave his life for the Christian faith.

people.name: Varus and the six monks with him
people.summary: A fourth-century soldier in Egypt who stood with six imprisoned monks and joined them in their crowning under Maximinus Daia.

people.name: Vendemanius the Hermit of Bithynia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Bacchus of Mar Saba
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Barlaam of Caesarea in Cappadocia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Christopher the Sabbaite
people.summary: Eighth-century monk of the Lavra of Mar Saba slain by the Saracens for the cause of Christ.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Elessa of Kythira
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Venerable-martyr George the Persian
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Macarius of Mount St. Auxentius
people.summary: Eighth-century monk near Constantinople slain for the cause of the holy icons.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Michael, Abbot of Zovia, and the thirty-six monks with him
people.summary: An eighth-century abbot of a monastery near Sebaste in Armenia and his community, slain together for refusing Islam.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Pansophius of Alexandria
people.summary: A third-century ascetic of Alexandria, son of the prefect Nilus, scourged to death for his Christian confession.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Theocteristus, Abbot of Pelekete in Prusa
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Theocteristus, Abbot of Pelekete Monastery
people.summary: An eighth-century abbot of a monastery near Prusa in Bithynia, scourged in the cause of the holy icons.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Theocteristus, Abbot of Pelekete near Prusa
people.summary: An eighth-century abbot of a monastery in Bithynia, scourged for the cause of the holy icons under Constantine V.

people.name: Venerable-martyr Theodora of Vasta
people.summary: A maiden of tenth-century Peloponnese who lived disguised as a soldier and at her death was buried where a chapel and a stand of trees still grow upon her grave.

people.name: Venerable Sergius of Radonezh
people.summary: The discovery in 1422 of the incorrupt relics of Sergius of Radonezh thirty years after his repose.

people.name: Veronica, healed of the issue of blood
people.summary: The woman of Caesarea Philippi whom the Lord healed at the touching of His garment, named in tradition Veronica.

people.name: Victor and Sosthenes of Chalcedon
people.summary: Two soldiers at Chalcedon converted by the courage of St. Euphemia who shared in her crown.

people.name: Victor and Stephanida of Damascus
people.summary: A Christian soldier of Damascus and the young wife of a fellow soldier converted at the sight of his courage, both slain together for the faith.

people.name: Victor, Dorotheus, Theodoulus, and Agrippa of Synnada
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Victor Stratelates (Photinus) and Joses, sons of Photini
people.summary: Two sons of the Samaritan woman who under Nero shared in their mother's martyrdom for the name of Christ.

people.name: Victor, Zoticus, Zeno, Acindynus, and Severian of Nicomedia
people.summary: Five Christians of fourth-century Nicomedia slain together under Diocletian.

people.name: Victorinus, Victor, Nicephorus and five companions of Corinth
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Victorinus, Victor, Nicephorus, Claudius, Diodorus, Serapion, and Papias of Corinth
people.summary: Seven Christians of Corinth slain together for the name of Christ under Decius.

people.name: Vincent of Lerins
people.summary: Fifth-century monk of the island monastery of Lerins in Gaul whose Commonitorium gave the Church its rule of catholic teaching — what has been believed everywhere, always, and by all.

people.name: Vincent of Spain, the Deacon of Saragossa
people.summary: An early-fourth-century deacon of Saragossa in Spain who endured every torment under Dacian the governor and reposed at last in the consolation of an angel.

people.name: Virgin-Confessor Drosida of Antioch with five virgin-martyrs
people.summary: The daughter of Emperor Trajan who at her secret baptism led five fellow consecrated virgins to martyrdom; she gave up her own soul in penance for forty days at her father's discovery.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Agnes of Rome
people.summary: A maiden of Rome at thirteen beheaded under Diocletian for refusing the betrothal her wealth had been arranged to seal.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Anastasia of Rome
people.summary: A third-century maiden of Rome who, raised in a convent of consecrated virgins, was beheaded under Valerian for the name of Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyr and Deaconess Apollonia of Alexandria
people.summary: A third-century deaconess of Alexandria who, when the pagan mob set fire to her funeral pyre, leapt into it of her own will rather than blaspheme.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Antonina of Nicaea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Apollonia of Alexandria
people.summary: An elderly deaconess of Alexandria in the persecution under Decius who at the pagan mob's threat of fire walked of her own will into the flames rather than blaspheme.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Basilissa of Nicomedia
people.summary: A nine-year-old girl of Nicomedia who endured fire and the beasts unharmed in 309 and at length reposed in peace at her prayers.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Cercyra of Corfu
people.summary: Daughter of the king of Corfu converted at the witness of Jason and Sosipater and slain by her father for the name of Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Charitina of Amisus
people.summary: A virgin of fourth-century Amisus on the Black Sea coast who suffered under Diocletian for her confession.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Cyriaca (Kyriake) of Nicomedia, with six holy virgin-martyrs
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Domnina of Syria
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Dorothea of Caesarea, with the lawyer Theophilus
people.summary: An early-fourth-century maiden of Caesarea in Cappadocia who at her execution sent roses from paradise to the mocking lawyer Theophilus, who was at once converted and crowned with her.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Ephigenia of Ethiopia
people.summary: An Ethiopian princess of the first century baptized by the apostle Matthew, who at length received the martyr's crown.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Erotheis of Cappadocia
people.summary: An early Cappadocian maiden who, kindled with love for Christ, came out victorious over the flames in which she was given to die.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Eugenia of Rome
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Euphrasia of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Eupraxia of Tabennisi
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Euthalia of Sicily
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Fausta of Cyzicus, with Evilasius and Maximus
people.summary: A thirteen-year-old maiden of fourth-century Cyzicus who at her endurance converted the priest Evilasius and the magistrate Maximus, all three crowned together.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Hermione of Ephesus
people.summary: A daughter of the apostle Philip the Deacon, who reposed at Ephesus after long labors of healing and prophecy.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Ia of Persia and the nine thousand with her
people.summary: A captive of fourth-century Persia who under Sapor II led many to Christ and was at length beheaded with thousands of her converts.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Irais of Alexandria
people.summary: A maiden of Alexandria beheaded under Diocletian after long torture for the name of Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Jerusalem of Byzantium
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Kyra (Cira, Shirin) of Persia
people.summary: Sixth-century Persian noblewoman of the royal house slain by her family for converting to Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Lucia of Syracuse
people.summary: An early-fourth-century maiden of Syracuse in Sicily, beheaded under Diocletian after her own betrothed had denounced her, the patroness of those with diseases of the eyes.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Lucy of Campania with twenty-four companions
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Manatho of Scythopolis
people.summary: A maiden of fourth-century Scythopolis in Palestine burned alive for refusing the sacrifice to idols.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Melissa of Marcianopolis
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Myrope of Chios
people.summary: A maiden of Chios under Decius who having given honorable burial to the martyr Isidore was found out and beaten to death by the soldiers responsible for the body.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Paula of Byzantium
people.summary: A young Christian woman who at the witness of Lucillian gathered the blood of the martyrs for honorable burial and was beheaded for it.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Pelagia of Antioch
people.summary: A fifteen-year-old maiden of Antioch in 303 who, surrounded by soldiers come to defile her, ran up to the rooftop and threw herself into the river rather than lose her purity.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Pelagia of Tarsus
people.summary: A third-century maiden of Tarsus betrothed to the emperor's son who, refusing the union to keep her vow to Christ, was placed in a heated bronze bull and gave up her soul in song.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Pherbutha of Persia, with her sister Mekadoshta and a maidservant
people.summary: A fourth-century maiden of the Persian court, sister of the patriarch Symeon Bar Sabbae, slain together with her household under Sapor II.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Philothea the Wonderworker of Pamphylia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Potamia of Alexandria the Wonderworker
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Potamiaena of Alexandria, with seven companions including Basilides
people.summary: A young Christian maiden of Alexandria under Septimius Severus given over to boiling pitch; her witness converted the soldier Basilides who shared in her crown.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Rhais of Alexandria
people.summary: A maiden of fourth-century Alexandria who suffered under Diocletian for the name of Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Sandukht of Armenia
people.summary: A first-century daughter of the king of Armenia converted at the apostle Thaddeus's preaching and beheaded by her father for the name of Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Seraphima (Serapia) of Antioch
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Susanna of Rome
people.summary: A late-third-century niece of Pope Caius of Rome beheaded under Diocletian for refusing the emperor's son in marriage.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Theodora and Martyr Didymus the Soldier of Alexandria
people.summary: A young maiden of Alexandria sent to a brothel and rescued by Didymus the soldier, both beheaded together for the name of Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Theodora of Palestine (Theodosia of Tyre)
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Theodosia of Constantinople
people.summary: Eighth-century nun of Constantinople torn to pieces by the iconoclast soldiers of Leo III the Isaurian when she fastened herself to the bronze gate of the Chalke to defend the icon of the Lord.

people.name: Virgin-martyr Theodosia of Tyre
people.summary: An early-fourth-century maiden of Tyre who at Caesarea in Palestine saluted condemned Christian confessors and was given over to the same fate.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Alexandra, Claudia, Euphrasia, Matrona, Juliana, Euphemia, and Theodosia of Amisus
people.summary: Seven Christian maidens of fourth-century Amisus on the Black Sea coast martyred together under Maximinus Daia.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Alexandra, Tecusa, Claudia, Phaine, Euphrasia, Matrona, and Julia of Ancyra
people.summary: Seven aged consecrated virgins of Ancyra in Galatia drowned together under Diocletian for refusing the rites of Artemis.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Archelais, Thecla, and Susanna of Salerno
people.summary: Three Christian maidens of southern Italy slain together under Diocletian for refusing the pagan rites.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Charissa, Nika, Galina, Callista, Nunechia, Basilissa, Theodora, and Irene of Corinth
people.summary: Eight Christian maidens of Corinth slain together with Leonidas of Nea Epidavros.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Cyrenia and Juliana of Cilicia
people.summary: Two Christian maidens of fourth-century Cilicia beheaded together for the name of Christ under Maximian.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Ennatha, Valentina, and Paula of Palestine
people.summary: Three maidens of fourth-century Palestine slain together at Caesarea for the name of Christ.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Irene, Agapia, and Chionia of Aquileia
people.summary: Three sisters of Aquileia under Diocletian who in their parents' country house had hidden away copies of the Scriptures and refused to surrender them.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Libya, Leonis, and Eutropa of Palermo
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Libya, Leonis, and Eutropia of Palmyra
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Martha and Mary with their brother the monk Lykarion
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Martha, Mary, and Lycarion of Tanis
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Rhipsima, Gaiana, and their thirty-five companions
people.summary: A Roman noblewoman who with her abbess and companions fled to Armenia from Diocletian and were beheaded there by Tiridates before his own conversion.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Tecusa, Alexandra, Claudia, Matrona, Polactia, Euphrosyne, Julia, Euphrasia, Faina, and Athanasia of Ancyra
people.summary: Ten consecrated virgins of fourth-century Ancyra in Galatia drowned together under Diocletian for refusing the rites of Artemis.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Thekla, Mariamne, Martha, Mary, and Ennatha of Persia
people.summary: Five Persian nuns beheaded together under Sapor II for refusing the worship of fire.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Valeria, Kyria, Martha, Mary, and Marcia of Caesarea in Palestine
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Virgin-martyrs Zenaida and Philonilla of Tarsus
people.summary: Two kinswomen of the apostle Paul who in first-century Cilicia took up the medical art for the sake of the poor and at length received the crown of martyrdom together.

people.name: Vitalis of Gaza
people.summary: An aged monk of seventh-century Gaza who in scandalous appearance lived among the women of the city to call them to repentance.

people.name: Vitalius and Valeria of Milan
people.summary: First-century husband and wife of Milan, parents of Gervasius and Protasius, who together received the crown of martyrdom.

people.name: Vitalius of Ravenna
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Vitus, Modestus, and Crescentia of Lucania
people.summary: A young boy of Lucania in southern Italy, his tutor, and his nurse, all martyred together under Diocletian.

people.name: Equal-to-the-Apostles Great Prince Vladimir of Kiev
people.summary: Grandson of Olga, baptized at Cherson in 988 and called the Enlightener of the Rus' for bringing the whole nation into the Christian faith. He abolished capital punishment and opened the storehouses of his treasury to the poor.

people.name: Vladimir Yaroslavich and his mother Anna of Novgorod
people.summary: An eleventh-century prince of Novgorod and his Swedish mother Ingegerd, the lady of Yaroslav the Wise, who together rest in the cathedral of Holy Wisdom there.

people.name: Vulkian
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Wenceslaus, Prince of Bohemia
people.summary: Tenth-century prince of Bohemia raised by his grandmother Ludmilla in the faith, treacherously slain at the church door by his brother Boleslaus.

people.name: Woman-martyr Heliconis of Thessalonica
people.summary: A third-century maiden of Thessaloniki tortured and beheaded for the name of Christ.

people.name: Woman-martyr Thomais of Alexandria
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Women-martyrs Kyriaki, Domnina, and Domna
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Xanthippe and Polyxena of Spain
people.summary: Two sisters of first-century Spain, disciples of the apostles, who labored in evangelism in the days of the apostolic preaching.

people.name: Xenia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Blessed Xenia of St. Petersburg, Fool-for-Christ
people.summary: Wife of a Russian colonel widowed young, who gave her wealth to the poor and took up the unkempt clothes and behavior of a fool-for-Christ, wandering St. Petersburg for forty-five years in voluntary poverty and unceasing prayer. The patroness of the homeless and of those who pray for their husbands.

people.name: Xenia of Rome
people.summary: A fifth-century Roman noblewoman who fled her arranged marriage to found a convent in Caria under an assumed name.

people.name: Xenia the Wonderworker of Kalamata
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Xenophon, his wife Maria, and their sons Arcadius and John
people.summary: A senator of Constantinople in the fifth century, his wife, and their two shipwrecked sons — each presumed the others lost and entered monastic life independently, only to be reunited in a vision at the holy places of Jerusalem.

people.name: Xenophon, Founder of Xenophontos Monastery on Mount Athos
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zabulon and Susanna, parents of St. Nina of Georgia
people.summary: A fourth-century nobleman of Cappadocia and his wife, who at the dedication of their daughter Nina to the Lord parted to lives of prayer in the desert and the holy places.

people.name: Zacchaeus of the Seventy, Bishop of Caesarea
people.summary: The publican of Jericho whom the Lord called down from the sycamore tree, who in the apostolic preaching became bishop of Caesarea in Palestine.

people.name: Zacchaeus the Deacon and Alphaeus the Reader of Caesarea
people.summary: A deacon of Gadara and a reader of Caesarea who together openly confessed Christ before the prefect and were beheaded.

people.name: Zachariah, Patriarch of Jerusalem
people.summary: Seventh-century patriarch of Jerusalem who saw his city taken by Khosrau II of Persia and was led away into Persian captivity.

people.name: Zachariah the Cobbler and his wife Mary
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zachariah the Recluse of Egypt
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zacharias of Nicomedia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zebinas, Germanus, Nicephorus, and Antoninus of Caesarea
people.summary: Four young men of Caesarea in Palestine who in 308 of their own will came forward to confess Christ and were given to the beasts and the sword.

people.name: Prophet Zechariah, father of John the Baptist
people.summary: The aged priest in the temple to whom Gabriel announced the conception of the Forerunner, slain by Herod's soldiers between the temple and the altar when he would not betray the hidden child.

people.name: Zenaida the Wonderworker of Caesarea
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zeno and Zenas his servant of Philadelphia
people.summary: A wealthy Christian of Philadelphia in Arabia and his servant, both crowned together under Diocletian.

people.name: Zeno, Eusebius, Neon, and Vitalis of Corfu
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zeno the Hermit
people.summary: Fifth-century hermit of Antioch, a disciple of Basil the Great and later anchorite of the Antiochene wilderness.

people.name: Zeno the Hermit of Egypt, the Wonderworker
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zeno the Postman of Antioch
people.summary: A fourth-century courier of the emperor Valens who at his discharge sold his goods and retired into a tomb-cell near Antioch for forty years of prayer.

people.name: Zenobius and his sister Zenobia
people.summary: A brother and sister of Cilicia, both physicians who served the poor, beheaded together under Diocletian after enduring the rack and the gridiron.

people.name: Zenon
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zenon, Bishop of Kyrenia in Cyprus
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zenon, Bishop of Maiuma in Gaza
people.summary: Fourth-century bishop of the port-city of Gaza in Palestine.

people.name: Zenon, Chariton, and Archontius
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zoe of Bethlehem and the Virgin Photini
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zosimas, Bishop of Babylon Fortress in Egypt
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zosimas, Bishop of Syracuse
people.summary: Seventh-century bishop of Syracuse, raised against his will from the monastic life to the see.

people.name: Zosimas of Cilicia, Bishop of Babylon in Egypt
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zosimas of Palestine
people.summary: Sixth-century priest-monk of the Jordan wilderness who found St. Mary of Egypt and at her death buried her.

people.name: Zosimas of Phoenicia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zosimas the Hermit and Athanasius the Commentarisius of Cilicia
people.summary: A desert anchorite and a prison-warden whom his prisoner converted, who together took up the ascetic life in Cilicia.

people.name: Zosimas the Hermit of Cilicia
people.summary: A fourth-century anchorite of the wilderness of Cilicia who suffered under the pagan persecutors.

people.name: Zosimas the Soldier of Apollonia
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zoticus, Bishop of Comana
people.summary: Commemorated in the Orthodox Synaxarion.

people.name: Zoticus the Presbyter, Feeder of Orphans
people.summary: A fourth-century Roman noble who came to Constantinople with Constantine and there gave his wealth and his days to gathering and feeding the city's orphans.
